summary: DHH comments on the state of Python-Cocoa integration on Mac OS X, noting that the bridge between Python, Java, and Objective-C makes it impractical. He expresses desire for a high-end Mac setup, humorously soliciting donations. This is an early, casual blog post reflecting his interest in both Python and Apple hardware before his Ruby on Rails era.
key_points: Python's integration with Apple's Cocoa framework was hampered by too many bridge layers between Python, Java, and Objective-C
DHH was a Python enthusiast before becoming known for Ruby on Rails
He expressed strong interest in Apple hardware early on, foreshadowing his long association with the Mac platform
money_quotes: So far the snazzy user interface framework on the Mac isn't really useful for Python wrestlers.
Too much bridge jumping from Python to Java to Objective-C and back again.
I'd *really* like to have a Mac by the time Python gets the full handle on Cocoa.
key_insight: In 2002, DHH was a Python enthusiast frustrated by the complexity of cross-language bridges for Mac development—an early signal of his preference for developer-friendly tooling that would later define his work with Ruby on Rails.
